author: barnabasbusa
content:
```I queried cl-3's REST API for data-column sidecars at every early slot:

┌─────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│          Slot           │ cl-3 /eth/v1/beacon/data_column_sidecars/{slot} │
├─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 30, 34 │ NOT_FOUND                                       │
└─────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

So no, cl-3 does not have data-column sidecars for slots 0–7 (or any early slot up through 34). Those early slots were either empty (no blob txs at chain start) or pruned. The current chain head is well past slot 100, but cl-3 returns NOT_FOUND even for slots within the standard DA retention window.```

author: barnabasbusa
content:
```
Why lighthouse penalised each peer type

Three distinct scoring rules tripped:

1. Other lighthouse node (cl-3 ↔ cl-6 at 10:02:30, mutual ban, score -100): both did a CustodyBackfill data_column_sidecars_by_range slots=8, start_slot=0 to each other; both responded Resource unavailable: columns pruned within boundary; both treated the response as malicious and slammed -100. Symmetric self-inflicted ban.
2. Prysm peers (cl-1, cl-4 — repeated kicks): invalid_gossip_proposer_preferences (−10 per offense, threshold −30 triggers forced disconnect). Lighthouse considers prysm's Gloas builder/proposer-preferences gossip spec-invalid. Score decays back to "healthy" so prysm reconnects, then trips again — cl-3's log shows cl-1 / cl-4 being kicked every ~30s for half an hour.
3. Lodestar peers (cl-2, cl-5): NotEnoughResponsesReturned — lighthouse asked for N data columns by-root, lodestar returned fewer than N. Lighthouse classifies as Low Tolerance Error (single offence kicks).
```

author: 0xunclebill
content: i think its a timing issue, for some reason we are trying to fetch columns before we see the envelope

author: 0xunclebill
content: peers return 0 columns (they havent seen/custodied the columns yet). the bid indicates that the slot should have columns and so we penalize

author: barnabasbusa
content: so point 2 here where prysm got downscored due to invalid gossip proposer preferences was caused by a lagging head

author: barnabasbusa
content: due to DA check missing

author: 0xunclebill
content:
I belive the full path is

- we receive an attestation for a block we havent seen yet (`UnknownHeadBlock` message in LH)
- we trigger an `UnknownBlockHashFromAttestation` which creates a pending lookup
- we fetch the block, see that the bid has kzg commitments. so we try to fetch columns
- peers dont have the columns yet so they return an empty response and we penalize

- _[IGNORE]_ The block with root `preferences.dependent_root` has been seen (via

```https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/gloas/p2p-interface.md?plain=1#L411-L413
